title: Shri Dwarkadhish Temple, Dwarka
intro: The Shri Dwarkadhish Temple, also known as the Jagat Mandir, is located in the city of Dwarka, Gujarat. This temple is dedicated to Lord Shri Krishna and is a vital part of the sacred 'Char Dham' pilgrimage in Hinduism.
description:
Shri Dwarkadhish Temple – Dwarka, Gujarat
Introduction
The Shri Dwarkadhish Temple, also known as the Jagat Mandir, is located in the city of Dwarka, Gujarat. This temple is dedicated to Lord Shri Krishna and is a vital part of the sacred 'Char Dham' pilgrimage in Hinduism.
Situated on the banks of the Gomti River, this temple is famous for its historical grandeur and architectural beauty. Every year, millions of devotees visit here to seek the blessings of Lord Krishna.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Dwarkadhish Temple (Jagat Mandir)
 Location: Dwarka, Devbhoomi Dwarka District, Gujarat 361335, India
 Presiding Deity: Lord Krishna (Dwarkadhish)
 Significance: One of the Char Dhams
 Nearest Major City: Jamnagar (approx. 145 km)
History and Significance
Dwarka is considered the workplace and ancient capital of Lord Shri Krishna. According to religious beliefs, Lord Krishna left Mathura and established Dwarka as his own city.
The temple is believed to be approximately 2,500 years old. In Hinduism, Dwarka is considered one of the seven cities that grant Moksha (liberation). The temple spire is particularly famous for its architecture and the flag (Pataka), which is changed several times a day.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air
 Jamnagar Airport: Located approx. 145 km from Dwarka. You can reach Dwarka from the airport via taxi or bus.
🚆 By Train
 Dwarka Railway Station: The city has its own railway station and is well-connected to major cities across India.
🛣️ By Road
Dwarka is well-connected by road to major Gujarat cities like Ahmedabad, Jamnagar, and Rajkot. Bus and taxi services are readily available.
Best Time to Visit
October to March is the most favorable time for travel.
Special Occasion:
 Janmashtami: The temple is filled with special festivities on this day, attracting a large number of devotees.
Temple Timings
🕒 6:30 AM to 1:00 PM and 5:00 PM to 9:30 PM.
(Timings may vary on special festivals and occasions.)
Nearby Attractions
 Bet Dwarka: The place believed to be where Lord Krishna met his friend Sudama.
 Rukmini Devi Temple: An ancient temple dedicated to Devi Rukmini.
 Gomti Ghat: The holy bank of the Gomti River where devotees take a dip.
 Sudama Setu: A famous pedestrian suspension bridge built over the Gomti River.
Dress Code
 Wearing modest and traditional clothing is mandatory while entering the temple.
 Maintain discipline and decorum.
Facilities for Devotees
 Darshan and Puja arrangements
 Prasad counters
 Security arrangements
 Dharmashalas and hotels for accommodation
 Local transport services
Conclusion
The Shri Dwarkadhish Temple is not just a religious site but a center of unwavering faith in Lord Shri Krishna. The spiritual energy of Dwarka and its rich culture provide peace to the heart of every devotee. A pilgrimage to Dwarka is a direct experience of devotion and the grace of Lord Krishna.

faq.q1: To which deity is the Dwarkadhish Temple dedicated?
faq.a1: This temple is dedicated to the Dwarkadhish (King of Dwarka) form of Lord Shri Krishna.
faq.q2: Where is the Dwarkadhish Temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in Dwarka, Devbhoomi Dwarka district, Gujarat.
faq.q3: Of which pilgrimage is the Dwarkadhish Temple a part?
faq.a3: It is a major Dham (abode) of India's Char Dham Yatra.
faq.q4: What is the best time to visit Dwarka?
faq.a4: The period from October to March is considered ideal for the pilgrimage.
faq.q5: Which places can one visit near Dwarka?
faq.a5: Bet Dwarka, Rukmini Devi Temple, Gomti Ghat, and Sudama Setu are the major places.
